decisions.decision: Implement `/ws/markets/{id}/prices` as a typed market-price stream that sends an immediate snapshot on connect and subsequent price-state updates shaped around the current read model: `marketId`, `status`, `marginals`, `seq`, `emittedAt`, `approxFlag`, plus optional terminal-event attribution metadata when the update is mutation-driven.
alternatives_rejected: Stream raw journal events only and force the frontend to reconstruct displayed prices from event payloads
Emit trade-result payloads only with no canonical market snapshot
Keep the socket silent until the next mutation instead of sending a connect-time snapshot

decisions.decision: Publish price messages only for accepted state transitions that change the displayed market state, which currently means `ProbabilityEdit` and market resolution updates; rejected commands and accepted `EventTrade` writes that leave `market.marginals` unchanged do not emit `/prices` updates.
alternatives_rejected: Broadcast every accepted terminal event even when `status` and `marginals` are unchanged

decisions.decision: Stay on the current stdlib server stack, add the WebSocket handshake/connection management around `BayesHandler`, and switch the serving runtime to `ThreadingHTTPServer` so long-lived sockets do not block concurrent REST traffic.
alternatives_rejected: Migrate the backend entrypoint to a different HTTP runtime
Add a third-party Python WebSocket framework for this task
Keep the single-threaded `HTTPServer` runtime while holding open live socket connections

decisions.decision: Treat `/ws/markets/{id}/prices` as a public read-only active-market subscription: reject connect attempts for non-active markets, allow already-open sockets to receive the terminal resolution update if the market resolves, and then close the socket cleanly.
alternatives_rejected: Require `X-Bayes-Agent-Id` or other auth on the read socket
Allow initial subscriptions to resolved or closed markets
Keep the socket open indefinitely after sending the terminal resolution state

decisions.decision: Keep React Query polling as the fallback source of truth while adding WebSocket-driven cache patching: the client reconnects with bounded backoff and refetches REST data on reconnect or disconnect instead of implementing sequence-aware resume in v1.
alternatives_rejected: Make the WebSocket authoritative and remove polling
Implement sequence-aware resume and missed-event catch-up on the initial rollout

decisions.decision: Verify the feature at both layers: backend integration tests will exercise a real socket handshake and live update ordering against the threaded server, and frontend tests will mock `WebSocket` to cover reconnect handling, cache patching, and fallback refetch behavior.
alternatives_rejected: Rely only on backend socket tests
Rely only on frontend mocked-WebSocket tests

steps.task: Add backend WebSocket infrastructure in `backend/server.py`: threaded server startup, upgrade detection for `GET /ws/markets/{id}/prices`, frame encode/decode helpers, per-market subscriber registry, active-market connect validation, initial snapshot serialization, and service-index exposure of the new socket route.

Wire the live-price broadcaster into the existing market mutation path so accepted price-changing writes publish ordered snapshots using the journal `seq`/`approxFlag` metadata, and resolution emits a final terminal update before subscriber cleanup.

Extend frontend API/types with the live-price message contract and a market-price subscription hook that opens the socket, patches the `useMarket` cache, invalidates dependent market queries when `seq` advances, and triggers REST refetch on reconnect/backoff paths without removing the existing polling interval.

Integrate the subscription hook into the market detail route so active markets receive live probability/status updates while inactive markets and disconnected sessions continue to render from the existing REST query state.

Add backend socket integration coverage for connection acceptance/rejection, ordered update delivery, reconnect-after-disconnect, and resolution-close behavior; add frontend Vitest coverage for cache patching and reconnect fallback; then run the targeted suites and commit the finished implementation.
